task: Write a Python function range_summary(nums) taking a list of integers, possibly unsorted with duplicates, and returning a string of the distinct values in ascending order, collapsing consecutive runs into 'start-end' and joining pieces with commas, so [1, 2, 3, 5] becomes '1-3,5'. A single number stays a single number, never a range. An empty list returns ''. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The function correctly handles the edge case where an empty list is input.
trace: First, I sorted the list and removed duplicates by converting it to a set. Then, I iterated through the sorted list and collapsed consecutive runs of numbers into ranges. If a number was not part of a range, it remained as a single number. Edge case handled: if there are no distinct values in the list, the function returns an empty string ''.
